title: Silky Midnight Mexican Chicken Wings
description: A floral and silky midnight-craving Mexican dish featuring chicken wings prepared with sous-vide for a gluten-free-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: Sous-vide bags
ingredients.notes: Choose silicone or parchment paper bags for sous-vide cooking.

ingredients.name: Chicken wings
ingredients.notes: Fresh, bone-in chicken wings from a trusted source.

ingredients.name: Poblano peppers
ingredients.notes: Roasted and diced for flavor infusion.

ingredients.name: Lemon wedges
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed lemon juice for dipping the chicken wings.

instructions: Preheat your sous-vide machine to 52°C (126°F). Pat dry the chicken wings and season generously with salt and pepper.
Place the seasoned chicken wings in the sous-vide bags, ensuring each wing is fully submerged. Cook for 3 hours at 52°C (126°F), turning halfway through cooking time to ensure even heat distribution.

Meanwhile, prepare a simple marinade by mixing sesame oil, vegetable oil, and minced garlic cloves (about 2-3 cloves). Heat the mixture over medium-high heat until it just begins to simmer. Reduce the heat to low and let it cool for about 10 minutes before adding the poblano peppers.

Carefully slice the cooled marinade into small cubes and add them along with the cooled oil, sesame seeds, chopped cilantro, diced poblano peppers, a pinch of cayenne pepper, and a drizzle of Mexican crema. Stir well to combine all ingredients into a savory sauce.

After removing chicken wings from sous-vide, pat off excess marinade. Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F) and place the chicken wings on a wire rack lined with parchment paper or aluminum foil in an oven-safe dish.

Brush both sides of the chicken wings with cooled sauce mixture and bake for about 25 minutes, flipping them once midway through cooking time. Keep an eye on browning; if needed, brush more sauce until golden brown.
Serve immediately with Oaxaca cheese and freshly squeezed lemon wedges.
